content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
Novenber 26 | 1-990
Bob Allen
city Attorney's office
city of whittier
L3230 Penn Street
whittier, cA 90602-]-772
Re: Your Request for Advice
our !'ile No. A-9 0-7 01
Dear Mr. AI len:
This is in response to your letter requestiirg advice with
respect to the confl ict-of- interest provisions of the Political
nef6rn Act.1/ In our telephone conversation of November 16, 1990,
you stated that you were requestinq forrnal advice on behalf of
ivnittier city councilmember Robert Henderson. (See, Regulation
18329 , copy encLosed. )
OUESTION
llay councilmenber Henderson partic5.pate in a city council
decisioi concerning the award of a contract to specialty Vehicles,
which was the low Eiaaer on the contract, lrhere specialty Vehicles
wiII be represented by an employee, Ms. Nancy Munoz, who has been
a source of income to Councilmember Henderson?
CONCLUSTON
If the governmental decision will affect Ms. Munoz's salary
or other j-ncorne or assets by $r,ooo or more, councilmember
Henderson rnust disqualify hirnself fron participatinq in the
decision. However, if the only financial effect of the decision
will be on Specialty Vehicles, the councilmember wiII not have a
conflict of interest with respect to the decision.
!/ 
covernment code sections 81ooo-91o15. All statutory refer-
ences are to the Governnent code unless otherwise indicated' 
com-
rnission regulations appear at 2 Californj.a code of Regulations
Section 18ooo, et seq. AII references to regulations are to Title
2, Divisi-on 6 of the Calj,fornia code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 o (916)322-5660


Our File No. A-90-70■
Page 2
FACTS
Robert Henderson is a city councilmember in the city of
Whittier. 
Councilmember Henderson is also a }ife insurance agent
in the city. 
In the course of doing business in the jurisdiction,
the counci lmenber recej-ved incorne of 5250 from l.Is. Nancy Munoz.
The city council is currently considering the award of a
contract to Specialty Vehicles, the low bidder, for the city's
purchase of five trolleys. 
Ms. Munoz is enployed by speciaLty
Vehicles and wil,1 be representing Specj.alty Vehicles before the
city council with respect to the trolley contract. 
You stated in
your supplenental materials that Ms. Munoz has no ownership
interest in Specialty Vehicles, and you do not know whether she
wi]l receive a commission if the contract is approved. You also
stated the city council is requi.red by 1aw to accept the lowest
qualified bidder.
You have asked whether councilmember Henderson therefore has
a confLict of interest which tril,L require hin to disqualify
himself frorn participating in decisions concerning the trolley
contract .
ANALYSIS
Section 871-oo of the Act prohibits any public official 
from
making, participating in rnaking, or otherwise using his or her
official 
position to influence a governmental decision in which
the official 
has a financial interest. 
A 'rpublic officj'altt is
defined in Section 82048 and Reg'ulation 18700 (copy enclosed) as
every natural person who j,s a nember, officer, employee, or
consultant of a state or Locaf governnent agency. This definition
woul-d include a menber of the Whittier City Council.
Your supplenental materials dated November l-6, 1990, state
that the city council as a natter of 1aw must accept the lowest
bid. If the vote is purelv rninisterial, such that the city
council has no discretion with respect to the terms of the
contract or the acceptance of the 1o$/ bidder for the contract, the
decision may not be considered a governmental decision which could
Iead to a conflict of interest under the Act. 
(Regulation
18700(d), copy enclosed.) However, lre do not have sufficient
j-nforrnation about the lega1 requirements concerning the bid to
reach a conclusion on this point. 
consequently, I have enclosed
the foll-owing letters on ministerial decisions for your
inforrnation: cano Advice Letter, No. I-90-190; Klein Advice
Letter, No. A-84-152; Sperrv Advice Letter, No. A-83-235.
Section 87103 specifies that a public official 
has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable


Our Fi■e No. A-90-70■
Page 3
that the decision wilt have a materj-al financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the
official 
or a member of his or her immediate family or on:
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than l-oans by a commercial lending institu-
tj-on in the regular course of business on terms
available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars
($250) or more in val,ue provided to, received by or
promised to the public official- within 12 months
prior to the tine when the decision is made.
Any person or business which has made any pa)ment to an
official 
in the past 12 months is a source of income to the
official 
for the purPoses of Section 87103. Thus, purchasers of
insurance policies from the Councilmember Henderson are sources of
income to tne councilmernber and wilL continue to be potentially
disouafifvinq economic interests for L2 rnonths after receipt of
tne'incoml.2/ 
Pursuant to Section 87Lo3, counciLmenber Henderson
may not participate in any governmental decision that !'/iLl
reasonably foreseeablv have a rnaterial financial effect on an
economic interest.
whether the financial conseguences of a decision are reason-
ably foreseeable at the tine a governmental decj-sion is made
deplnds on the facts of each particular case. An effect is
considered reasonabLy foreseeable if there is a substantial
l-ikefihood that it will occur. certainty is not required-
Ilorrever, if an effect is only a mere possibility, 
it is not
reasonably foreseeable. (In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC ops. l-98,
copy enclosed. )
Materialitv
In addition, the effect on the councilnember's source of
income must be material in order to require disqualif ication. 
The
comnission has adopted differing guidelines to determine whether
an effect is rnaterial-, depending on the specific circunstances of
each decision. For example, where a source of incorne is directly
before the city council, as an applicant or the subject of the
't 
Regulation La7o4.3 (copy enclosed) provi.des guidelines for the
attribution of commission income for conflict-of- interest
purposes. Regulation L8704.3 provides that for an insurance
broker or agent, aII of the following are sources of income in a
given sale: 
(a) The insurance company providing the policy;
(b) The person purchasing the policy,' and (c) The brokerage
f irrn, agency, company, or other business entity through which the
broker or agent conducts business. Moreover, for purposes of
deterrnining whether disqua l if ication is required, the fuI1 gross
value of any cornrni.ssion income for a speci-f ic transaction sha11 be
attributed to each source of income in that saLe or transaction.


Our Fi■e No. A-90-70■
Page 4
decision, Regulation 18702.1(a) (copy enclosed) provides that the
effect of the decision on the source of incone is deemed material
and disqua l if ication is required.3/ 
(cornbs Advice Letter,
No. A-89-177 , copy enclosed.)
AccordingT to your facts Ms. Munoz is not directly before the
city councit, but is only there in a representative caPaci.ty.
Wheie a source of income appears in a representative capacity, we
have advised that the person is not directly involved in a
decision. (Gi1] Advice Letter, No. A-85-252, coFy enclosed.)
Thus, only specialty Vehicles is directly involved in the contract
decisj.on, and since Specialty vehicles has not been a source of
income to the counci hnernber, their involvernent does not result in
a conflict of interest.
However, even where a source of income is not directly before
the official's 
agency, disquaJ. ification is stiIl 
required if the
source of income will- be indirectly naterially affected by the
decision. Regulation ta702.6 (copy enclosed) provides:
The effect of a decision is rnaterial as to an
j,ndividual who is a source of income or gifls to an
official 
if any of the following applies:
(a) The decision wilL affect the
individual's income, investments, or other
tangible or intangible assets or liabilities
(other than real property) by $1,000 or nore;
or
(b) The decision wil,l, affect the
individual's real- property interest in a rnan-
ner that is considered material under Section
L87 02.3 0r Section La702.4.11
consequently, if the decision will affect Ms. I'tunoz's salary
or other interests by 51,000 or more, councilmenber Henderson may
not participate in the decision. For example, if Ms. ltlunoz will
receive a cornrnission as a result of approval of the contract of
S1,ooo or more, the effect of the decision on Ms. Munoz iri1l be
3/ A source of incorne is directly before the city council when
the source initiates the proceedinq by f j-1ing an application,
cIaim, appeal, or simil,ar request, or j-s a named party in, or the
subject of, the proceeding. A person or business entity is the
subject of a proceeding if a decision invol.ves the issuance,
renerral-, approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or
other entitlement to, or contract with, the subject person or
business entity. (Regulation L8702.1(b).)
Regulations La702.3 and 18702.4 have been encl-osed for future
reference.


Our Fi■e No. A-90-70■
Page 5
material and the councilmember may not participate- 
However, if
the only effect will be on Specialty vehicles, the councifmember
will not have a conflict of interest. 
(Harron Advice Letter,
No. A-84-288, copy encLosed. )
Finalty, even if Councilnenber Henderson determines that he
has a financial interest that will be materially affected by the
decision, he nay still 
participate in the decision if the effect
on the councilmember's financiaL interest is not distinguishable
from the effect on the public generally. For the itpublic
general.lyrr exception to apply, the decision must affect the source
of income in substantially the same manner as it will affect a
significant segment of Whittier. (Regulation 18703, copy
enclosed.) 
Since any effect on Ms. Munoz that will arise frorn
the transaction will be unique to her, the public generally
exception would not apply under these facts.
Should you have any further questions regarding this natter
please feef free to contact ne at (916) 322-59OL-
s incerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
SH : Jww: aa
Encl osures
John w. wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
